title: Beam interaction behavior
principles: Beam behavior interprets the interaction properties of a component already confirmed as beam delivery and must not repeat the general delivery classification.
Beam range defines the maximum supplied reach of the component but does not establish tracking, accuracy, contact consistency, or target acquisition behavior.
Contact requirements, tick frequency, and target-retention behavior must be interpreted only when explicitly supplied.
Fire rate must not be substituted for beam tick frequency unless the source explicitly defines that relationship.
Multishot must not be interpreted as additional visible beams, independent contacts, or separate status opportunities without a documented beam-specific interaction.
Beam-specific behavior must remain separate for each attack mode or component when their range, contact, or application models differ.

defined_reach: The supplied beam range establishes the maximum documented reach of the evaluated component.
Practical contact within that range remains dependent on separately supplied interaction evidence.
maintained_contact: The component requires continued valid contact to maintain its supplied application behavior.
Do not infer how quickly application stops, resets, or resumes unless those rules are documented.
discrete_beam_application: The beam produces explicitly defined discrete damage or application events.
Use the supplied event frequency without replacing it with displayed fire rate.
multiple_beams_defined: The supplied mechanic explicitly produces more than one beam or beam contact.
Preserve the target, damage, critical, status, and retention behavior of each beam when supplied.
limited_definition: Beam delivery is confirmed, but only part of its reach, contact, or application behavior is defined.
undetermined: Beam delivery is confirmed, but its specific contact, tick, acquisition, or multishot behavior cannot be determined from the supplied data.

charge_behavior_present: Treat charge as an attack-rhythm property and do not redefine it as beam application behavior.
damage_ramp_present: Treat progressive damage change as a separate mechanic and do not infer that it represents charge accumulation.
chaining_present: Treat chaining as a separate propagation mechanic and do not classify chained targets as radial coverage or additional beams without explicit evidence.
radial_component_present: Evaluate the radial component independently from the beam component.

exceptions: Do not infer beam tick frequency from fire rate or beam classification.
Do not infer perfect, uninterrupted, or automatically tracked contact.
Do not infer additional beams or independent applications from multishot alone.
Do not infer target-retention, contact-reset, acquisition, or damage-retention rules when absent.
